content_of_post_to_comment: My mum bought me the same perfume I bought her once when I was 16. I’m wearing it today and it seems to be filling me with memories. Most of them good, to be honest. I’m making new memories in it. She is flying back home after I told her I’m not moving back. I’m going to wear it today with my friends while we make Momo together. Then I’m going to wear it to a board games group tomorrow evening. And a writing group on Saturday. I’m going to wear this perfume and my mum’s love everywhere I go. But I will make sure I’m always going forward.
comment: Perfume as portable memory raises a subtle question: when you keep wearing inherited scent, are you curating the past or letting it curate your present choices without noticing?
